  · -- The zeros at s = -2, -4, ... are simple, as can be seen from the functional equation.
    -- At these trivial zeros, the simple pole of the Gamma function Γ(s/2) in the completion
    -- Λ(s) = π^(-s/2) Γ(s/2) ζ(s) must be cancelled by a simple zero of ζ(s).
    -- Since Λ(s) = Λ(1-s) and Λ(1+2n) ≠ 0 for Re(1+2n) > 1, the zero of ζ(s) is simple.
    sorry
  · exact (hcs hcs_in).elim
